title: Fix duplicate sv.verbatim.module when filename is shared
body:
This is a fix for https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/9131. The mistake was:

- **Before PR**: In BlackBoxReader if two blackboxes are found to use the same filename, we compute the LCA of the directories and make just one emit.file op for it.
- **Ater PR**: In BlackBoxReader, if two blackboxes are found to use the same filename, we compute the LCA and attach the  output file/content info to both `firrtl.extmodule`s. Then, in LowerToHW, each of these lowers to sv.verbatim.

The fix implemented here tracks the filenames of `firrtl.extmodule` that have been lowered to `sv.verbatim.module`. If a `firrtl.extmodule` is encountered with a duplicated filename, an additional `sv.verbatim.module` is not created. Instances of the duplicated extmodule will reference the pre-existing `sv.verbatim.module`.
